artifact.content_preview:
From: dave.chen@ssc-spc.gc.ca
To: alice.tran@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Cc: carol.nguyen@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Subject: Opportunity: Wi-Fi Infrastructure Upgrade During Network Migration
Date: March 7, 2026

Alice, Carol,

While reviewing the network migration plan for Site A, I wanted to flag
an opportunity that I think we should discuss with the steering committee.

Given that our contractors will already be on-site and the network
infrastructure will be partially disrupted during the Catalyst 9500
switch deployme

extracted_tasks.text: Discuss Wi-Fi infrastructure upgrade opportunity with the steering committee
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: I wanted to flag an opportunity that I think we should discuss with the steering committee.

extracted_tasks.text: Upgrade Wi-Fi access points to Catalyst 9120 series across Sites A, B, and C
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: Upgrading to the Catalyst 9120 series during the same migration window would avoid a separate disruptive maintenance activity

extracted_tasks.text: Obtain steering committee approval for the expanded scope and budget
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: Could we add this to the scope and get steering committee approval before the March 18 window?

extracted_tasks.text: Decision: Use the Catalyst 9500 switch deployment window for concurrent infrastructure upgrades
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: this would be an ideal time to also upgrade the Wi-Fi access points across all three data centre facilities.

metadata.subject: Opportunity: Wi-Fi Infrastructure Upgrade During Network Migration

alerts.detected_ask: Upgrade Wi-Fi access points (Cisco Aironet 2800 to Catalyst 9120 series) across all three data centre facilities during the Catalyst 9500 switch deployment window, at an estimated additional cost of $85,000–$110,000 and 9 additional days of timeline impact.

in_scope: Decommissioning of Sites B and C physical infrastructure
Migration of all server workloads from Sites B and C to Site A
Physical server rack consolidation and cable management at Site A
Environmental systems (HVAC, UPS, fire suppression) optimization at Site A
Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites
Network segmentation reconfiguration per updated security zones
BGP routing table migration and validation
Migration of all on-premise Active Directory domain controllers to consolidated infrastructure at Site A
AD schema extension and forest restructuring
Group Policy Object (GPO) consolidation and testing
Integration testing with downstream applications dependent on AD
Weekly status reports to SSC Project Management Office
out_of_scope: End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices) — addressed under NGIS Phase 4
Application modernization or re-platforming — no changes to application code, architecture, or hosting platform; lift and shift only
GC-Cloud workload migration to public cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GC-Cloud) — addressed under GC-Cloud Wave 4
End-user training and change management beyond project communications — responsibility of client departments
Procurement of new application licenses — software licensing changes resulting from platform migration
Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative
deliverables: D-001: Project Management Plan — Due: October 31, 2024
D-002: Technical Design Document (TDD) — Due: December 15, 2024
D-003: Security Assessment and Authorization (SA&A) Package — Due: January 31, 2025
D-004: Migration Runbook (Sites B and C) — Due: February 28, 2025
D-005: User Acceptance Test (UAT) Plan — Due: March 10, 2026
D-006: Site A Consolidation Complete (Go-Live) — Due: April 30, 2026

D-008: Project Closure Report — Due: June 30, 2026
constraints: Total approved budget is $2,400,000 CAD inclusive of all fees, disbursements, and applicable taxes
Expenditures beyond the approved budget require a formal budget amendment approved by the Director General of IT Infrastructure
Budget variances exceeding 15% require submission to Treasury Board
Go-Live (D-006) must be achieved no later than April 30, 2026
Final project closure date is June 30, 2026 (end of fiscal year 2025–2026)
Extensions beyond June 30, 2026 require ADM-level approval
All scope, schedule, or budget changes must follow the SSC Project Change Management Process and require written approval from the Project Sponsor
Compliance required with ITSG-33: IT Security Risk Management — A Lifecycle Approach

llm_calls.content:
```json
{
  "alignment_score": 0.15,
  "drift_type": "scope_expansion",
  "detected_ask": "Upgrade Wi-Fi access points (Cisco Aironet 2800 to Catalyst 9120 series) across all three data centre facilities during the Catalyst 9500 switch deployment window, at an estimated additional cost of $85,000–$110,000 and 9 additional days of timeline impact.",
  "baseline_reference": "IN SCOPE: Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites; Network segmentation reconfiguration per updated security zones. OUT OF SCOPE: Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative (by analogy, network hardware upgrades beyond defined scope). CONSTRAINTS: Total approved budget is $2,400,000 CAD inclusive of all fees, disbursements, and applicable taxes — expenditures beyond approved budget require formal budget amendment approved by the Director General of IT Infrastructure; All scope, schedule, or budget changes must follow the SSC Project Change Management Process and require written approval from the Project Sponsor.",
  "suggested_action": "Do not proceed informally. Dave Chen must submit a formal Change Request through the SSC Project Change Management Process before any commitments are made to TechSupplier or the steering committee. The Change Request must document: (1) full scope delta — Wi-Fi AP replacement at Sites A, B, and C is not referenced anywhere in the approved scope baseline; (2) budget impact of $85,000–$110,000 against the $2,400,000 approved ceiling, including whether this pushes total expenditure beyond the 15% variance threshold requiring Treasury Board submission; (3) schedule impact of up to 9 additional working days against the April 30, 2026 Go-Live constraint (D-006), which is only 54 days from the date of this email — the PM must assess whether this is achievable without jeopardizing Go-Live; (4) security implications under ITSG-33, as new wireless infrastructure may require an updated SA&A assessment (D-003 was due January 31, 2025 and may not cover new AP hardware); (5) written approval from the Project Sponsor prior to any steering committee discussion or vendor engagement. The informal conversation already held with TechSupplier should be noted as a process concern — no procurement commitments can be made outside the formal change control process.",
  "reasoning": "The request to upgrade Wi-Fi access points is a clear scope expansion. The approved scope baseline explicitly limits network hardware work to the Cisco Catalyst 9500 switch deployment and associated network segmentation and BGP migration activities. Wireless access point infrastructure is not mentioned anywhere in the in-scope items. While the rationale offered — co-location of contractors, EOL hardware, efficiency gains — is operationally reasonable, it does not constitute an approved scope item. The estimated cost of $85,000–$110,000 represents a 3.5%–4.6% budget increase on its own; depending on current budget consumption, this could trigger the 15% variance rule requiring Treasury Board submission. The 9-day timeline addition is a material risk given the hard April 30, 2026 Go-Live deadline (D-006) with no approved extension mechanism below ADM level. Additionally, the informal vendor engagement described in the email bypasses the SSC Project Change Management Process, which is a governance compliance issue independent of whether the change itself is ultimately approved. The alignment score of 0.15 reflects that while the work is physically adjacent to in-scope activities and involves the same contractor presence, it is substantively outside the defined deliverables, budget envelope, and hardware scope of the project."
}
```

parsed_json.detected_ask: Upgrade Wi-Fi access points (Cisco Aironet 2800 to Catalyst 9120 series) across all three data centre facilities during the Catalyst 9500 switch deployment window, at an estimated additional cost of $85,000–$110,000 and 9 additional days of timeline impact.
parsed_json.baseline_reference: IN SCOPE: Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites; Network segmentation reconfiguration per updated security zones. OUT OF SCOPE: Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative (by analogy, network hardware upgrades beyond defined scope). CONSTRAINTS: Total approved budget is $2,400,000 CAD inclusive of all fees, disbursements, and applicable taxes — expenditures beyond approved budget require formal budget amendment approved by the Director General of IT Infrastructure; All scope, schedule, or budget changes must follow the SSC Project Change Management Process and require written approval from the Project Sponsor.
parsed_json.suggested_action: Do not proceed informally. Dave Chen must submit a formal Change Request through the SSC Project Change Management Process before any commitments are made to TechSupplier or the steering committee. The Change Request must document: (1) full scope delta — Wi-Fi AP replacement at Sites A, B, and C is not referenced anywhere in the approved scope baseline; (2) budget impact of $85,000–$110,000 against the $2,400,000 approved ceiling, including whether this pushes total expenditure beyond the 15% variance threshold requiring Treasury Board submission; (3) schedule impact of up to 9 additional working days against the April 30, 2026 Go-Live constraint (D-006), which is only 54 days from the date of this email — the PM must assess whether this is achievable without jeopardizing Go-Live; (4) security implications under ITSG-33, as new wireless infrastructure may require an updated SA&A assessment (D-003 was due January 31, 2025 and may not cover new AP hardware); (5) written approval from the Project Sponsor prior to any steering committee discussion or vendor engagement. The informal conversation already held with TechSupplier should be noted as a process concern — no procurement commitments can be made outside the formal change control process.
parsed_json.reasoning: The request to upgrade Wi-Fi access points is a clear scope expansion. The approved scope baseline explicitly limits network hardware work to the Cisco Catalyst 9500 switch deployment and associated network segmentation and BGP migration activities. Wireless access point infrastructure is not mentioned anywhere in the in-scope items. While the rationale offered — co-location of contractors, EOL hardware, efficiency gains — is operationally reasonable, it does not constitute an approved scope item. The estimated cost of $85,000–$110,000 represents a 3.5%–4.6% budget increase on its own; depending on current budget consumption, this could trigger the 15% variance rule requiring Treasury Board submission. The 9-day timeline addition is a material risk given the hard April 30, 2026 Go-Live deadline (D-006) with no approved extension mechanism below ADM level. Additionally, the informal vendor engagement described in the email bypasses the SSC Project Change Management Process, which is a governance compliance issue independent of whether the change itself is ultimately approved. The alignment score of 0.15 reflects that while the work is physically adjacent to in-scope activities and involves the same contractor presence, it is substantively outside the defined deliverables, budget envelope, and hardware scope of the project.
